Buy My Car · Site Guide 01 / 29

Site guide

How to run your
website & console

A plain walkthrough of every part of your Adam Hall Buy My Car site: the brand pages your customers know, the cars-for-sale marketplace, and the console you run it all from.

23 features · Website & dealer console · Prepared by Jewell Projects

Scroll, or use your arrow keys  ↓

Two halves

Everything lives in two places

Your website

What your customers see and use.

  • Home, in the full brand
  • How it Works & Buy My Car
  • Adam’s story, Listen, Contact
  • Cars for sale & each car’s page
  • The 5-minute valuation flow
  • Finance, compare, saved, FAQ

Dealer console

Where you run the business, at /admin.

  • Dashboard
  • Adam AI
  • Submissions
  • Inventory
  • Enquiries & finance
  • Site copy & analytics

Part one

Your website

Fourteen pages your customers move through — the brand pages they know, plus the cars-for-sale marketplace.

Your website · 04

The home page

Your front door, in the full Adam Hall brand. The hero, trust bar and story sections come straight from adamhallbuymycar.com.au.

  1. 1The big "Call Adam" button and your real number sit right up top.
  2. 2The trust bar backs it up: 5-star reviews, 27 years, same-day payment.
  3. 3Scroll on for the value-my-car and buy-my-car sections, cars for sale, testimonials and FAQs.

TipThe nav now carries both sides of the business: selling pages and Cars for Sale.

Your website · 05

Cars for sale, on the front page

Live stock, right in the middle of the home page. It updates itself whenever you publish or sell a car.

  1. 1The four newest published cars show as cards.
  2. 2Each card links to the car’s own page.
  3. 3"View all cars for sale" takes buyers to the full lot.

Your website · 06

How it works

The step-by-step of selling to you, exactly as the brand site tells it.

  1. 1Walks a seller through the process from call to same-day payment.
  2. 2Finishes with the FAQ accordion and a call-to-action.

Your website · 07

Buy My Car

The valuation landing page. Its own focused layout, built to convert.

  1. 1A seller fills in their details and car.
  2. 2The form checks everything, then takes them into the 5-minute valuation flow.
  3. 3That flow lands in your Submissions queue in the console.

TipOn the old site this form went nowhere. Now it feeds your real pipeline.

Your website · 08

Adam’s story

Who you are and why people trust you — word for word from the brand site.

  1. 1Your background, your approach, your face.
  2. 2Reachable from the menu on every page.

Your website · 09

Listen

Your "What’s Your Car Worth?" radio segments, playable on the site.

  1. 1Episodes play right on the page.
  2. 2Great proof for new visitors: you’re the one on 4CRB.

Your website · 10

Contact

Your phone and a message form, in the brand layout.

  1. 1Callers ring 0404 290 617 straight from the page.
  2. 2Messages land in your console under Enquiries.

Your website · 11

Cars for sale

The full lot — the page buyers spend the most time on.

  1. 1Filter by make, price or body type; sort to taste.
  2. 2Each card shows price and the specs that matter.
  3. 3Click any car for its full page.

TipThe watchlist lets a buyer get a message the moment a matching car lands.

Your website · 12

A car’s own page

The full story on one car. Where a browser turns into a buyer.

  1. 1Photo gallery up top, full specs below.
  2. 2"Adam’s take" is your honest word on the car.
  3. 3PPSR status shows it’s clear; buttons let them enquire or book a look.

Your website · 13

The valuation flow

The working 5-minute sell flow behind the Buy My Car page.

  1. 1Rego, a few details, photos, contact — done.
  2. 2It drops straight into your Submissions.
  3. 3The seller gets a private link to track your offer.

Your website · 14

Finance

A repayment calculator and a lead form for buyers who need finance.

  1. 1Buyers play with deposit and term to see weekly figures.
  2. 2Their enquiry lands in your Finance tab in the console.

Your website · 15

Compare cars

Line up to three cars side by side so a buyer can decide with confidence.

  1. 1Tap the scales icon on any car to add it.
  2. 2Price, specs and weekly repayment sit in one table.
  3. 3Best price and lowest kays are highlighted.

Your website · 16

Saved cars

A buyer’s shortlist, built with the heart icon on any car.

  1. 1Tap the heart to save a car.
  2. 2The shortlist stays on their device — no account needed.

Your website · 17

FAQ

The common questions, answered up front — so you field fewer of the same calls.

  1. 1Grouped by topic and easy to scan.
  2. 2Edit any answer yourself in Site copy.

Part two

Dealer console

The nine tools you run the whole business from, all behind one login.

Dealer console · 19

Logging in

The private door to your console. Only accounts you’ve allowed can get through.

  1. 1Go to your site address followed by /admin.
  2. 2Enter your email and password.
  3. 3No public sign-ups — you decide who gets in.

TipChange the demo password before you go live.

Dealer console · 20

The dashboard

Your morning glance at the state of the business, in four numbers.

  1. 1New submissions, open enquiries, live listings, sold this month.
  2. 2The activity feed shows what’s happened most recently.
  3. 3"View site" jumps you back to the public site.

Dealer console · 21

Adam AI

Your own AI offsider. Ask it anything about the business, or get it to write for you.

  1. 1It already knows your live stock, submissions and how the site works.
  2. 2Ask for social captions, a reply to a tricky buyer, or pricing thoughts.
  3. 3Past chats save in the side rail; "New chat" starts fresh.

Dealer console · 22

Submissions

Every car people want to sell you — from the Buy My Car page and the valuation flow — in one queue.

  1. 1Open a submission to see the details and photos.
  2. 2Make your offer; the seller is updated automatically.
  3. 3Move it through the stages as the deal progresses.

Dealer console · 23

Inventory

Your live stock — add, edit, publish and mark sold.

  1. 1Add a car with photos, specs and price.
  2. 2Publish to put it on the site (and the home page strip).
  3. 3Mark it sold when it’s gone and the site updates.

Dealer console · 24

Enquiries

Buyer questions, book-a-look requests and contact-page messages, all together.

  1. 1See who’s asking and which car they mean.
  2. 2Reply and mark it handled so nothing slips.

Dealer console · 25

Finance leads

Everyone who asked about finance, in one list to work through.

  1. 1Each lead shows what the buyer’s after.
  2. 2Work them top to bottom.

Dealer console · 26

Site copy

Change the words on your site yourself. This is the one that saves you the most.

  1. 1Edit headlines, page copy, contact details and hours.
  2. 2Hit save and it’s live on the site straight away.
  3. 3No developer, no waiting.

TipThis is where your street address, hours and licence number go.

Dealer console · 27

Analytics

A read on what’s happening — what people look at and what they act on.

  1. 1See views and which cars draw the most interest.
  2. 2Track enquiries so you know what’s working.

A day on Buy My Car

Five minutes, top to bottom

  1. 1
    Glance at the Dashboard. New submissions, enquiries, what sold.
  2. 2
    Work your Submissions. Review, make offers, keep sellers moving.
  3. 3
    Clear your Enquiries. Reply to buyers, mark them handled.
  4. 4
    Keep Inventory fresh. Add new stock, mark sold cars sold.
  5. 5
    Ask Adam AI. Captions, replies, a second opinion on price.

Before you go live

A short checklist

  • Put your street address, hours and licence number in Site copy.
  • Change the demo login password.
  • Connect the live Google reviews feed.
  • Point adamhallbuymycar.com.au at this site.
  • Have the legal pages checked over.

Anything unclear? Jewell Projects is a message away.
